QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL MEASURED AT COCORAHS TX-WB-5 SITE. RANCH OWNER NEXT DAY ESTIMATED HAIL COULD HAVE BEEN LARGER JUST EAST AND NORTH OF COCORAHS SITE, BASED ON STRIPPED LEAVES AND SMALL MESQUITE BRANCHES BROKEN AS WELL AS HOLES PUNCHED THROUGH CACTUS.

THERE WAS AN EF-0 TORNADO THAT OCCURRED AT THE TOWN OF OLD SAN PATRICIO WITH WINDS OF 65 TO 75 MPH. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH WIDTH OF 25 TO 50 YARDS AND A PATH LENGTH OF A HALF MILE LONG. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS WERE DOWN ALONG THE PATH AND A COUPLE OF OLDER TREES WERE KNOCKED OVER. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED NEAR THE CORNER OF NOPAL STREET AND COUNTRY ROAD 1664.
SEVERAL PUBLIC REPORTS OF WATERPOUT OVER NUECES BAY.
MEASURED ON HOME WEATHER SYSTEM. ALSO REPORTED SEVERAL WILD FIRES DUE TO LIGHTNING IN THE AREA AND MUCH OF VICTORIA WAS LEFT WITHOUT POWER.
HAM OPERATOR REPORTED DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL IN SAN PATRICIO COUNTY AROUND 5 MILES NORTH OF MATHIS ADJACENT TO THE SAN PATRICIO/LIVE OAK COUNTY LINE.

SAN PATRICIO SHERIFF RECIEVED A PUBLIC REPORT OF A VERY BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR FM136 AND HIGHWAY 188. ROOF DEBRIS FROM AN UNKNOWN STRUCTURE...POSSIBLY A BARN...WAS SEEN FLYING THROUGH THE AIR. THE SHERIFF OFFICE RESPONDED TO THE SCENE AND REPORTED NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE.
POWER POLES DOWN ON COUNTY ROAD 43 AND COUNTY ROAD 53 AS WELL AS COUNTY ROAD 43 AND FM 2444. REPORTED BY SHERIFFS OFFICE WHEN INFORMED THEM OF SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WARNING.
PUBLIC REPORTED TORNADO ALONG HIGHWAY 188
PUBLIC REPORTED TORNADO ALONG HIGHWAY 35
THE CORPUS CHRISTI NAVAL AIR STATION ASOS MEASURED A 51 MPH WIND GUST.
THE PACKERY CHANNEL TCOON SITE RECORDED A 44 MPH WIND GUST.

LAREDO FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED 10 INTERSECTIONS CLOSED IN THE CITY OF LAREDO DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THERE HAVE ALSO BEEN A LOT OF HIGH WATER RESCUES WITH STRANDED MOTORISTS. IN ADDITION...SEVERAL RESIDENTIAL HOMES SCATTERED THROUGHOUT THE CITY ARE FLOODED.
HIGHWAY 188 NEAR INTERSTATE 37 WAS IMPASSABLE.
SHERIFF OFFICE REPORTING ROAD CLOSURE AT DICKERSON AND CARR ROAD. WATER COVERING ROAD.
HIGH WATER RESCUE AT CARR RD AND CHARCO RD INTERSECTION.
POWER LINE DOWN AT COUNTY ROAD 81 AND COUNTY ROAD 40
44 MPH GUST REPORTED BY THE CORPUS CHRISTI MARINA WEATHER STATION TCOON.
40 MPH GUST REPORTED BY THE PORT ARANSAS C-MAN STATION.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED A DOWNBURST WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 60-70 MPH OCCURRED OVER REDFISH BAY AND MOVED NORTH ACROSS HIGHWAY 361. THE WINDS RIPPED THE PATIO ROOF OFF THE BAIT SHOP AT THE FIN AND FEATHER RV PARK. TWO POWER POLES WERE ALSO KNOCKED DOWN AND A VEHICLE WAS DAMAGED IN THE PARKING LOT.
MATAGORDA ISLAND RAWS

NUMEROUS POWER OUTAGES ACROSS THE AREA DUE TO DOWNED POWER POLES AND LINES WITH 8,300 CUSTOMERS WITHOUT POWER IN NUECES COUNTY. NWS SURVEY OBSERVED SEVERAL LARGE TREES...SIGNS...AND CARPORTS BLOWN DOWN IN THE NEIGHBORHOODS SURROUNDING THE NAVAL AIR STATION IN FLOUR BLUFF.
NAVY CORPUS CHRISTI
PACKERY CHANNEL
MALAQUITE BEACH NOS-NWLON

ARANSAS COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED MANY CITY STREETS FLOODED DURING THE EVENING AND OVERNIGHT HOURS. SEVERAL VEHICLES WERE STRANDED IN HIGH WATER INCLUDING A PATROL CAR. EIGHT HOMES WERE FLOODED WITH ONE HOME UP TO A FOOT DEEP NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LINDEN AND LADY CLAIRE. SCHOOLS WERE CLOSED TODAY AS A PRECAUTION.
PACKERY CHANNEL
BOB HALL PIER
BIRD ISLAND
